title: Department Of Engineering To Host Public Meeting For South Ave Bridge
summary: The City of Syracuse Department of Engineering will host a public meeting on March 24, 2026 to gather community feedback on the planned rehabilitation of the South Avenue Bridge over Onondaga Creek. The $-amount-undisclosed project includes replacing bearings, rebuilding sidewalks, repairing masonry, and resealing the deck; online comments accepted through April 10.

entity_refs.name: City of Syracuse Department of Engineering

political_signal.description: Mayor Owens frames bridge rehabilitation as critical infrastructure investment and public safety priority; positions project as preserving connectivity and community service.

processing_notes: Publication date discrepancy: header shows 'Published on March 20, 2026' but URL and content reference meeting on 'Tuesday, March 24'
No project budget or funding source disclosed in release
Seals Community Center location: Kirk Park, 300 W. Borden Ave confirmed as meeting venue
Public engagement mechanism: in-person meeting + online comments portal (syr.gov/south-ave-bridge)
Project scope clearly detailed: specific work items listed (bearings, sidewalks, masonry, deck sealing, painting)
Author listed as 'Unknown' - drafted by Department of Engineering or Office of Communications
